title: Apple surpasses Nvidia to become the world's most valuable company
summary: Apple's market value briefly surpassed that of chipmaker Nvidia, reclaiming its position as the world's most capitalized company.

title: Semiconductor stocks suffer worst week in over a year, dragging global markets
summary: A strong wave of selling in the tech sector, driven by the impact of Chinese artificial intelligence and demand doubts, causes stock index plunges.

title: Oil soars as Iran-US conflict escalates
summary: Continued attacks and tensions in the Middle East raise crude prices, benefit refineries, and reignite debate on energy security in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: SpaceX shares plunge after Starship test flight aborts
summary: The failed launch of the Starship rocket causes a drop in the company's stock, which was already suffering losses since its recent IPO.

title: Rise in cyberattacks against US companies: Abbott and Coca-Cola affected
summary: Ransomware incidents and a general increase in cyber threats hit major US corporations, though operations are not disrupted.

title: Trump Media proposes charging $100,000 per month for priority access to president's messages
summary: The company seeks to monetize the real-time publication of presidential statements, drawing criticism over privatization of official information.

title: Netflix falls on stock exchange after quarterly results, analysts see buying opportunity
summary: The streaming platform disappoints the market, but some investors believe the correction offers an attractive entry point.

title: Brussels pushes for creation of transnational European banks and Germany softens stance on Unicredit's bid for Commerzbank
summary: The EU presents a plan to encourage banking consolidation, while the German government reduces its resistance to the Italian bank's offer.

title: Travelers Companies shares lead gains on Wall Street
summary: The insurer surges in stock market, becoming one of the top performers in the Dow Jones and S&P 500.

title: ConocoPhillips rises in stock market, partners with BP to redevelop Kirkuk field in Iraq
summary: The US oil company announces its participation in the supergiant oil field project, boosting its stock price.

title: Japan's stock market falls dragged by semiconductor plunge
summary: The Nikkei index and futures record heavy losses in a week marked by risk aversion in the global tech sector.

title: Bonds rally amid risk aversion as Fed hints at reviewing monetary policy
summary: The yield on the 10-year US bond falls, while Fed President Jefferson indicates it might be appropriate to reconsider rates; the BoE also faces pressure.

title: Global stock markets end week lower due to geopolitical tensions and tech slump
summary: The Ibex, Dax, and Wall Street post weekly losses, pressured by the Middle East conflict and correction in the semiconductor sector.

title: Advance of Chinese AI Kimi shakes Silicon Valley, Nvidia seeks alternatives
summary: The Chinese model Kimi demonstrates performance close to Anthropic's, while Japanese companies consider switching to South Korean NPUs.

title: Warren Buffett announces gradual sale of all his Berkshire shares to donate to foundations
summary: The investor plans to divest from his own company over the next decade, while AI successfully analyzes his strategy.

title: Big tech companies intensify investments in artificial intelligence
summary: Meta, Alphabet, Intel, and Amazon launch or boost AI initiatives, as the sector debates who should bear the costs of this technology.

title: Bitcoin surpasses $65,000 and debates on cryptocurrency self-custody intensify
summary: The cryptocurrency reaches a new weekly milestone, while the advantages of self-custody wallets versus centralized platforms are analyzed.

title: South Korea to ban issuance of new single stock leveraged ETFs
summary: The South Korean financial regulator imposes restrictions on these high-risk products to protect investors.

title: Drone manufacturers anticipate 300% growth in US defense spending
summary: Companies in the sector raise their forecasts after the 2027 military budget is revealed, which includes a drastic increase in acquisitions.

title: Modi accelerates search for uranium and critical minerals for India's energy independence
summary: The Indian government intensifies exploration and acquisition missions for strategic resources to reduce external dependence.
